figures.gloss: The absolute, indivisible Creator and Sustainer of the heavens and the earth. He is unbegotten, unbegetting, and beyond all human comprehension, governing the cosmos entirely through His merciful and omnipotent will.
figures.facet: Tawhid (absolute oneness) is so uncompromising that associating partners with Him (shirk) is the ultimate theological corruption. He has no sons, no avatars, and no dualistic rivals; He is simultaneously utterly transcendent and closer to a person than their jugular vein.
figures.provenance: The Qur'an; Hadith Qudsi; classical Kalām (Islamic theology).

figures.gloss: The literal, uncreated speech of Allah, revealed sequentially to Muhammad through the archangel Jibril. It is not merely a record of divine inspiration but the infallible, phonetic presence of God's mind in human history.
figures.facet: Unlike Christianity where the Word became flesh, here the Word became text. The Arabic recitation itself is a sacramental act, placing the vibrational weight of divine reality directly into the human throat.
figures.provenance: The Qur'an; orthodox Ash'ari and Maturidi theology.

figures.gloss: The final messenger sent to humanity, concluding the long chain of Abrahamic prophethood. He bore the ultimate revelation, established the final covenant, and ascended through the heavens to the divine presence during the Isra and Mi'raj.
figures.facet: He is emphatically defended as purely human—he performs no salvific miracles by his own power and shares no divine essence. Yet his lived example (sunnah) serves as the perfect, eternal template for behavior, merging mundane daily hygiene with cosmic destiny.
figures.provenance: The Qur'an; Sahih Hadith collections; classical Sira (prophetic biographies).

figures.gloss: The cubic black stone sanctuary in Mecca, built by Abraham and Ishmael, serving as the qibla (direction of prayer) for all Muslims. It is the earthly reflection of the celestial house circumambulated by angels.
figures.facet: It contains no idol and no icon; believers prostrate toward an empty center. It acts as the gravitational core of a global ritual matrix, physically aligning millions of bodies synchronously every day.

figures.gloss: The entity who refused God's command to prostrate before the newly created Adam out of pride in his own fiery origin. Cast out, he acts as the tempter, permitted by God to test humanity until the Day of Judgment.
figures.facet: Iblis is not a fallen angel (who lack free will) but a jinn. His rebellion is not a genuine cosmic threat to God's throne but a permitted, strictly bounded trial. His fatal flaw was not desiring godhood, but racist pride ('I am better than him: You created me from fire and him from clay').

figures.gloss: The esoteric and exoteric leadership of the Muslim community, vested exclusively in the purified lineage of the Prophet through Fatima and Ali. The Imams are the infallible guides and living interpreters of the Qur'an's inner meaning.
figures.facet: While legislative revelation ended with Muhammad, divine guidance could not. The Imam serves as the cosmic pole (Qutb) without which the earth would shatter, ensuring a continuous, physical human bloodline carries the indispensable light of esoteric truth.
figures.provenance: Nahj al-Balagha; Usul al-Kafi; foundational Shia theology.

figures.gloss: The mystical Sufi state of 'annihilation' in God, where the seeker's individual ego and will are entirely subsumed into the divine presence, achieved through intensive dhikr (remembrance) and ascetic discipline.
figures.facet: It dances on the razor's edge of orthodoxy. While adhering to strict monotheism, it insists the ultimate realization of Tawhid is the obliteration of the 'I'—not becoming God, but realizing the individual self was always an illusion masking the One.
figures.provenance: Writings of Rumi, Ibn Arabi, Al-Ghazali; early Sufi manuals.

figures.gloss: The obligatory pilgrimage to Mecca, ritually reenacting the trials of Abraham, Hagar, and Ishmael. Pilgrims gather on the plain of Arafat in a vast rehearsal of the Day of Judgment.
figures.facet: It enforces a radical, physical egalitarianism. Kings and paupers wear the exact same unstitched white cloth, anchoring an abstract, transcendent monotheism to extreme physical exertion, heat, and visceral geographic specificity.
figures.provenance: The Qur'an; Sunnah; consensus of Islamic jurisprudence.

edges.note: The Qur'an is the uncreated speech of Allah, serving as His direct, active presence in the material world.
Muhammad is the unlettered human vessel perfectly chosen to receive and transmit the divine text.
Iblis represents the ultimate rejection of Allah's sovereign command, choosing personal pride over divine submission.
The Hajj is the active, kinetic ritual whose entire geographic logic is anchored by the physical structure of the Kaaba.
GOLD SEAM: The explicit, documented theological identification of the God of the Qur'an with the God of Abraham and Moses.
GOLD SEAM: Muhammad revolutionized the pre-Islamic High God of the Quraysh by stripping away all associated partner deities to assert strict monotheism.
GOLD SEAM: Structurally, the Qur'an occupies the exact theological space in Islam that Christ does in Christianity: the eternal, uncreated Word of God entering the physical world.
GOLD SEAM: Both prophetic figures explicitly claimed the title of the 'Seal of the Prophets', bringing a final revelation to correct the corrupted texts of previous traditions.
GOLD SEAM: The cosmic adversary who seeks to ruin humanity, though Islam strictly denies him the co-eternal, dualistic power held by the Zoroastrian destructive spirit.
GOLD SEAM: The literal integration of the pre-Islamic Meccan sacred center and its meteorite into the heart of the new monotheistic framework.
GOLD SEAM: Both systems locate ultimate liberation in the extinguishing of the illusion of the independent ego, though Sufism subsumes it into the Divine presence rather than a void.
GOLD SEAM: Both are fiery, primordial beings who interrupt the creator's intended cosmic order through a premature act of rebellious pride.
GOLD SEAM: Both represent the absolute, indivisible source of reality that entirely transcends human categorization and physical incarnation.
GOLD SEAM: The ultimate, non-dual reality underpinning all existence, a parallel drawn explicitly in later Persian and Indian Sufi metaphysics (Wahdat al-Wujud).
GOLD SEAM: The architectural and spiritual center of the world system, operating as the cosmic navel around which believers perform ritual circumambulation.
GOLD SEAM: The Prophet's Night Journey (Mi'raj) through the heavens parallels the ecstatic shamanic ascent, though here used to finalize the universal law of prayer.
GOLD SEAM: The Shia concept of a continuous, physically transmitted lineage of divine authority echoes foundational archetypes of an unsevered bloodline carrying world-sustaining power.
GOLD SEAM: The pilgrimage operates as a temporal return to the very beginning, ritually reenacting the primal actions of Abraham and Hagar to reset the believer's spiritual clock.
GOLD SEAM: The Jesus Prayer and Sufi dhikr are parallel disciplines of unceasing divine-name repetition dissolving the ego toward union — a long-noted structural kinship, not a documented borrowing.

GOLD SEAM: under Islam the iskoki survive by reclassification as aljanu — the Qur'anic jinn-class to which Iblis belongs — orthodoxy's own demonology becoming the cover under which the old pantheon persists.

GOLD SEAM: bori's map of its own Islamization — the order of Allah absorbed as the 'white' Muslim moiety of the pantheon while the pagan 'black' spirits keep the bush; a tension seam, not an equation.

GOLD SEAM: like bori's iskoki, the zayran live inside Islam as a species of jinn — Boddy's Sudanese hosts class them as the 'red wind' — Qur'anic demonology licensing a women's cult that Islamic reformers condemn.

GOLD SEAM: Ghulam Ahmad claimed only a derivative, non-law-bearing prophethood under Muhammad's seal — the subordination itself is the doctrine, and the reading of that seal is the exact wound Pakistan's anti-Ahmadi laws legislate.
GOLD SEAM: Both answer the same wound — how divine guidance continues after the founder — one through an infallible bloodline, the other through an elected caliphate held to be divinely guided.
GOLD SEAM: The Báb's initial 1844 claim was to be the gate of the Hidden Twelfth Imam — Twelver Shi'i expectation, sharpened by the Shaykhi school, is the soil the movement grew from.
GOLD SEAM: The Bayán is modeled on the Qur'an's station while explicitly claiming to abrogate its dispensation — successor scripture, not commentary.
GOLD SEAM: Every silsila terminates in the Prophet (most via 'Ali) — Sufism is Islam's interior discipline claiming direct descent, not a separate religion beside it.
GOLD SEAM: Fana, annihilation in the Beloved, is the terminus of dhikr practice — the parent shard already carries Sufism's crown concept; one seam, same body.
GOLD SEAM: The Sevener imamate descends directly from the Shi'i doctrine of divinely designated leadership, branching at Ja'far al-Sadiq's succession in 765 — proven doctrinal descent within Islam.

dossier.essence: The monotheistic religion founded on the prophethood of Muhammad (c. 570–632 CE) in Arabia, whose roughly two billion adherents (Muslims) hold that God (Allah) revealed the Qur'an to him through the angel Jibril, completing the line of Abrahamic prophecy. Its central claim is tawhid — the absolute oneness of God — and submission (islam) to God's will. It is divided principally into Sunni and Shia branches, with Sufism as its mystical dimension.
dossier.cosmos: God is the sole, indivisible creator, transcendent yet 'closer than the jugular vein,' who made the world and will judge it. History runs from creation to a final Day of Judgment when the dead are resurrected bodily and assigned to Paradise (janna) or Hell (jahannam) according to faith and deeds. Alongside humans, God created angels (lacking free will) and jinn (possessing it, made from fire); Iblis, a jinn who refused to bow to Adam out of prideful self-regard, is the permitted tempter until the end of time.
dossier.practice: Sunni Islam rests on the Five Pillars — the confession of faith (shahada), five daily prayers (salat) toward the Kaaba in Mecca, almsgiving (zakat), the Ramadan fast, and the Hajj pilgrimage — within a comprehensive law (sharia) derived by jurists from Qur'an and prophetic example. Community life is shaped by the mosque, the scholars (ulama), and emulation of Muhammad's sunnah in even mundane conduct; Shia Islam adds the authority of the Imams (Ahl al-Bayt), and Sufi orders pursue annihilation of the ego in God (fana) through remembrance (dhikr). There is no priesthood or sacrament in the Christian sense.
dossier.canon: The Qur'an, held to be the uncreated speech of God, is primary; it is supplemented by the Hadith (reports of Muhammad's words and deeds, in canonical collections such as Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim) and the Sira (prophetic biography), which together ground Islamic law and theology.
dossier.reflects: Uncompromising monotheism — divine oneness pressed so far that associating any partner with God (shirk) is the cardinal sin, and revelation enters the world as recited text rather than incarnate flesh.
sources: Marshall G. S. Hodgson, 'The Venture of Islam' (1974)
W. Montgomery Watt, 'Muhammad: Prophet and Statesman' (1961)
